items.summary: Sound built its reputation on concentrated, high-conviction bets in category-leading AI labs, while Kutcher's new fund appears to be chasing the layer underneath those companies — the infrastructure and energy that power them.

items.title: Sony announces end of PlayStation discs, parts of digital store in the same day
items.summary: “We will own nothing, it's truly sad.”

items.title: Best 4th of July deals you can shop ahead of this weekend’s celebrations
items.summary: The best 4th of July sales and deals are now in full swing at Best Buy, Walmart, Amazon, and elsewhere. Alongside a host official Independence Day events, with Prime Day bumping right up against the start of the early July sales, we also have a host of particularly notable hangover deals from the big Amazon event sitting next to a series of fresh price drops. Power stations and e-bikes, loads of PC deals and the giant Best Buy sale, Apple gear all-time lows, apparel, grocery items for the big cookout, and more all await below.

items.title: Neocloud Together AI raises $800M, leaps to $8.3B valuation
items.summary: The AI neocloud provider, which specializes in hosting open source models, last raised at a $3.3 billion valuation in early 2025.

items.title: A good little EV you won't be able to buy soon: The Volvo EX30 Cross Country
items.summary: Tariffs and anti-China policies killed this little Volvo in the United States.

items.title: Lime begins life as a public company after years of uncertainty
items.summary: The nine-year-old scooter and bike-share company has said it needs the funds to help pay down around $1 billion in liabilities.

items.title: Ukrainian charged in Germany over Nord Stream blasts
items.summary: Ukraine denies involvement in the case which may have serious implications for its relationship with Germany.

items.title: Enjoy bulk savings, fresh food, and perks with a 1-year membership to Sam’s Club for only $15 (Reg. $60)
items.summary: TL;DR: Grab a one-year Sam’s Club membership for $15 through July 5. New members can shop for bulk groceries, home essentials, electronics, and access extra savings on travel and services.

items.title: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
items.summary: Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.

items.title: First deal strikes Samsung’s 2026 Odyssey G7 32-inch 4K dual-mode gaming monitor at $300 off
items.summary: Over at Samsung, you’ll find the latest Odyssey G7 (G73SH) 4K 165Hz gaming monitor listed for $849.99 shipped. This particular listing also includes a $50 upgrade credit that auto-applies during checkout, essentially dropping the monitor down to $799.99 shipped from its usual price of $1,100. This is the first deal we’re tracking for this model, and it takes $300 off its price tag to mark a new best price to beat. Amazon currently has the same model listed for $850, meaning you’re missing out on the $50 upgrade credit for additional savings. This is one of the first dual-mode gaming monitors from Samsung that can switch between 4K and FHD resolutions, and you can learn more about it below.

items.title: Krafton settles with Subnautica 2 developer after drawn-out dispute over $250 million
items.summary: After a lengthy legal dispute, Krafton has settled with its subsidiary Unknown Worlds Entertainment, which is developing Subnautica 2, and will pay bonuses to the studio's staff, Bloomberg reports. The dispute began last year after Krafton pushed out Unknown Worlds' cofounders, Charlie Cleveland and Max McGuire, and its CEO, Ted Gill, ahead of a potential […]

items.title: Xbox testing disc-to-digital feature that digitizes a physical game collection
items.summary: Microsoft will likely soon follow Sony and stop the production of physical discs for Xbox games. But instead of leaving physical discs behind entirely, sources familiar with Microsoft's plans tell me the company has quietly been working on a disc-to-digital feature that will allow Xbox owners to digitize their existing physical game collections. Xbox employees […]

The apps await below.

items.title: Jackery’s 256Wh/80,000mAh Explorer 240D compact power station is down at its second-lowest $129 price
items.summary: The official Jackery Amazon storefront is offering its Explorer 240D 80,000mAh Compact Portable Power Station down at $129 shipped, beating out the direct website where it’s keeping to its $209 MSRP. Despite it carrying that $209 MSRP, we’ve seen it climb as high as $235 on Amazon in 2026, with discounts having previously gone as low as $129, until Prime Day gave us a newer $119 low. If you missed last week’s deal for that low, you can pick it up here at the next-best price, saving you $80 off the going rate while equipping your outdoor adventures with a far more compact and carriable power solution. Learn more below, or you can also shop from the brand’s Independence Day Sale lineup here.

We did see the Summit White model go for a touch less at Woot earlier this year, and this is the lowest we have seen this model fetch at Amazon. Head below for more details.

Buy for $25 (Reg. $36)

What we love

A lot of affordable travel power strips have meager Type-C ports (if any at all), but this unit packs a punch with 65W output in tow. It also has USB-A and AC at the ready, as well as a wind-up design that makes it easy to stow in your bag.

JOYROOM 125W USB-C Car Charger (2-pack)

Prime-exclusive discount.

What we love

You’re looking at not one, but two car chargers in this bundle. Each offers up to 60W USB-A and 65W Type-C charging for a combined output of 125W. The 65W output is plenty to top off most devices ranging from smartphones to tablets, a MacBook Air, and the list goes on.

items.summary: Amazon is currently offering the Greenworks 80V 42-inch CrossoverZ Cordless Electric Zero Turn Riding Lawn Mower with 6x 6.0Ah batteries + 600W wall charger for $4,699.99 shipped, matching the price we’re seeing directly from the brand’s website. Normally this all-encompassing package would run you $5,700 at full price, which we’ve seen discounted anywhere between $4,900 and $4,655, which was the low we spotted during last week’s Prime Day event. Today’s deal is a $1,000 markdown that gives you the next-best rate if you missed out last week, landing it just $45 above that low price.
